skill.name: python-executor
skill.description: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via inference.sh. Pre-installed: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, Playwright, MoviePy, Pillow, OpenCV, trimesh, and 100+ more libraries. Use for: data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, video creation, 3D model processing, PDF generation, API calls, automation scripts.
skill.summary: Execute Python code in a sandboxed environment with 100+ pre-installed libraries for data processing, web scraping, and automation.
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The external_commands detections are backtick syntax in shell examples, not actual code execution risks. Network detections are legitimate use of requests/BeautifulSoup for the documented web scraping functionality. No malicious patterns confirmed after semantic review. The skill properly documents its remote execution capability as a feature.

high_findings.title: Static Finding Evaluated as False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: The static scanner flagged '3.10' (Python version) as a weak cryptographic algorithm. No cryptographic algorithms are present in this skill. The skill is a code execution service that runs Python in a sandboxed environment.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Python version string '3.10' incorrectly triggered crypto detector. Semantic review confirms no cryptographic code exists.

high_findings.title: Static Finding Evaluated as False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
high_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'isolated subprocess' as network reconnaissance. This is security documentation stating the execution runs in an isolated subprocess for safety.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Word 'subprocess' in security documentation triggered incorrect detection. Context confirms this is describing sandboxed execution.

high_findings.title: Static Finding Evaluated as False Positive: External Command Execution
high_findings.description: The static scanner flagged backtick syntax in shell command examples as external command execution risks. These are documentation examples showing how to use the belt CLI, not actual code execution vulnerabilities.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick syntax in markdown code blocks (CLI examples) detected as shell execution. Semantic review confirms these are legitimate documentation examples.

high_findings.title: Static Finding Evaluated as False Positive: Python HTTP Libraries
high_findings.description: The static scanner flagged requests/BeautifulSoup usage as network risks. These are the documented, intended capabilities for web scraping functionality.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: requests and BeautifulSoup are core web scraping libraries. Detection is correct pattern but false positive in intent - web scraping is the documented feature.

content.user_title: Execute Python code with 100+ libraries for data processing and automation
content.value_statement: Need to process data, scrape websites, or generate images but lack local Python setup? This skill executes Python code in a secure sandboxed environment with 100+ pre-installed libraries including Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, and BeautifulSoup.
actual_capabilities: Execute arbitrary Python code in an isolated sandboxed environment with 8GB RAM (16GB option available)
Web scraping with requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, and Playwright for extracting data from websites
Data analysis using Pandas, NumPy, and SciPy for processing and transforming datasets
Generate visualizations with Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly saved as image files
Image manipulation with Pillow and OpenCV including resizing, cropping, and filters
Create videos with MoviePy including text overlays, transitions, and exports to MP4
limitations: CPU-only execution with no GPU support - cannot run ML/AI models or CUDA-dependent libraries
Timeout limited to 300 seconds maximum - long-running processes may be terminated
Non-interactive environment - cannot display plots with plt.show(), must use plt.savefig()
Limited filesystem access - only files in outputs/ directory are returned to the caller

use_cases.title: Data scientists analyzing datasets
use_cases.description: Process CSV files, generate statistical reports, and create visualizations without setting up a local Python environment.

use_cases.title: Developers automating web data extraction
use_cases.description: Build web scraping pipelines to extract product prices, article content, or competitor data from websites.

use_cases.title: Content creators generating visual assets
use_cases.description: Create charts, diagrams, edited images, or short videos programmatically for reports and presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Python execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this Python code: import pandas as pd; df = pd.DataFrame({'a': [1,2,3]}); print(df.describe())
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic data analysis with one-liner code snippets

prompt_templates.title: Web scraping task
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape the title and first paragraph from https://news.ycombinator.com using requests and BeautifulSoup

prompt_templates.title: Data visualization request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bar chart comparing sales data for products A, B, C with values 100, 150, 200 and save as chart.png

prompt_templates.title: Complex data processing pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Load data.csv, clean missing values, calculate monthly aggregates, create a line chart of trends, and export results to report.pdf
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-step data processing with file outputs

output_examples.input: Create a line chart showing temperature data for 7 days
output_examples.output: Successfully generated chart.png with a line plot showing temperatures: [72, 75, 78, 74, 71, 69, 73] degrees across Mon-Sun. File saved to outputs/ directory.

output_examples.input: Extract all article titles from a news website
output_examples.output: Found 15 article titles extracted from example.com. Top results: 'New AI Model Released', 'Tech Stock Market Update', 'Climate Report Summary'. All titles saved to outputs/articles.json

best_practices: Use plt.savefig() instead of plt.show() since the environment is non-interactive and cannot display plots directly
Save output files to the outputs/ directory so they are automatically captured and returned with the response
Set appropriate timeout values for long-running operations - increase from the default 30 seconds for complex computations
anti_patterns: Do not assume interactive input is possible - all code must be self-contained without user prompts or STDIN read
Avoid very long-running operations exceeding 300 seconds as they will be terminated by the timeout
Do not try to access local files outside the working directory - only files in outputs/ are returned to the caller

faq.question: What Python version is supported?
faq.answer: The sandbox runs Python 3.10 with over 100 pre-installed libraries including NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, requests, BeautifulSoup, Pillow, OpenCV, and MoviePy.

faq.question: Can I run machine learning models?
faq.answer: No, this is a CPU-only environment without GPU support. For ML models, use dedicated AI apps or services that provide GPU resources.

faq.question: How do I get my output files back?
faq.answer: Save files to the outputs/ directory in your Python code. Any files saved there are automatically detected and returned with the execution response.

faq.question: What is the maximum execution time?
faq.answer: Default timeout is 30 seconds, but you can configure it up to 300 seconds. For high-memory workloads, use the high_memory variant with 16GB RAM.

faq.question: Is the execution environment secure?
faq.answer: Yes, code runs in an isolated subprocess that prevents access to the host system. This is a sandboxed execution environment designed for safe code running.

faq.question: How do I use external APIs in my code?
faq.answer: You can use the requests library to make HTTP calls to external APIs. The sandbox has network access for web scraping and API integration tasks.
